translations.source_text: 周公拜乎前，魯公拜乎後。
translations.translation:
Cao Huan (Jingming)
曹奐 (景明)
Lived AD 246–303
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
Scroll 4 (Wei 4)
byZL181
onSeptember 7th, 2025

TheKingofChenliu[陳留王], taboo name[Cao] Huan, styledJingming, was the grandson of theMartialEmperorand the son of thePrinceofYan[燕王],[Cao] Yu. In the third year of theera [258], he was enfeoffed asTownship DukeofChangdao[常道鄉公] inAnci county[安次縣].
When theTownship DukeofGaogui Township[高貴鄉公] died, theExcellenciesandMinistersdiscussed to receive and enthrone theDuke. On the Jiayin day of the sixth month [27 June 260], he enteredLuoyang[洛陽] and met theAugust Empress Dowager. That day, he becameAugust EmperoratHall of Great Zenith[太極殿]. There was a grand amnesty and the era name was changed. People were gifted peerages, grain, and brocades, each with distinction.
***
On the Bingchen day of the sixth month [29 June] of the first year of theera [260], summer, theGrand General, theCivil Kingof the Sima clan, was to be advanced in position to becomeChancellor of State, enfeoffed asDukeof Jin [晉公] with his fief increased by two commanderies and merging with the previous to a total of ten, and granted the courtesy of having the Nine Bestowments. This was like a previous edict. Among his various younger relatives, those who were notMarquiseswere to all be enfeoffed asDistrict Marquisesand were to be gifted ten million cash as well as ten thousand bolts of silk. TheCivil Kingfirmly declined, putting this to an end. On the Jiwei day [2 July], the former Lady to theAstuteEmperorofHan,[Cao] Jie, died. TheEmperormourned her at theGarden of Lustrous Trees[華林園]. He sent a messengerbearing tallyto posthumously title theLadyas theAstuteandSolemnAugust Empress[獻穆皇后]. When she was buried, the rites for the vehicles and clothing were conducted like those of the erstwhileHan dynasty. On the Guihai day [6 July], the RightVice Secretariat Minister,Wang Guan, becameExcellency of Works.
In the tenth month, winter,[Wang] Guandied.
In the eleventh month, thePrinceofYan[燕王] petitioned to celebrate the winter solstice and declared himself a subject. An edict stated:
“Of the princes of antiquity, some of them did not have to declare themselves as subjects. ThePrinceshould follow that principle. Why will he not declare himself a subject in his petitions? And I shall have to reply to him! Those who inherit the main lineage of their clan should devalue their immediate kin, more so when what they are inheriting is more important! But if I treat him akin to a mere servant, and that makes me also feel uncomfortable. You should all make a verdict according to rites and rules. You are to strive to the fullest in this matter.”
Thedirecting officialspetitioned, believing:
“In the rites, there is nothing more venerable than honoring ancestors, and in the regulations, there is nothing more important than the orthodox law.Your Majesty, you have befitting virtue and auspicious luck, nurturing and governing a myriad of lands. You have received the onus of inheriting the main lineage of your clan and enhanced the foundation of the Three Progenitors. We lay prostrate to say that we believe that thePrinceofYan[燕王] is distinguished among your blood relatives. When he holds rank in his fiefdom, he is reverent and solemn, acting with respect and virtue, and so he surpasses the multitude of other states. According to the orthodox law, this establishes great submission, and so he should not be curbed. ButYour Majestysincerely should honor him with unconventional regulations and grant him the courtesy of not declaring himself a subject. Therefore, we subjects of yours deliberated. We believe that when thePrinceofYan[燕王] submits petitions, he should be allowed to do so in the past manner. As for the palace edicts that have been issued, some of them have contained pleasant greetings. If we may compare them to an analogy, then they are like the respects exchanged in a banquet. We can slightly polish your sagacious reverence for him by enhancing the formal address to show that we dare not be so sharp, and so we should state, ‘TheAugust Emperorrespectfully asks for the service ofYour Highness.’ As for decrees, they are the orthodox law of the state, and they are used by the court to distinguish official regulations and propagate guidelines and etiquette to the world. In following the proper method, they should therefore state, ‘The decree for thePrinceofYan[燕王].’ When edicts, decrees, memorials, and petitions mention thePrinceofYan[燕王], they should all shift the title to the next line.1In matters unrelated to aiding with sacrificial offerings at the ancestral temples, they all may not mention the name of thePrinceofYan. Memorials, petitions, letters, and the officials and people all may not infringe upon the taboo name of thePrince. This would display extraordinary etiquette for him, more so than the other nobility. Thus, this would adhere to the royal law of the regulation of honoring your ancestors, and this would conform to your sagacious reverence of being with a filial heart. These two things do not contradict each other, and they certainly accord with the rites. You should announce this everywhere and undertakeit.”•••
When these documents wrote the words of “PrinceofYan[燕王]”, they were shifted to the next column and placed at the top, called “top level” [上平] or “level shifting” [平抬], starting a new line.↩
On the Jiashen day of the twelfth month [23 January 261], a yellow dragon was seen in a well inHuayin county[華陰縣]. On the Jiaxu day [2 February 261], theColonel who Directs Retainers,Wang Xiang, becameExcellency of Works.
***
On the first lunar day of the fifth month [15 June] of the second year [261], spring, there was a solar eclipse.
In the seventh month, autumn, the foreign barbarians of Han and Yemaek inLelang[樂浪] each led their subordinates to come to court and pay tribute.
On the Wuyin day of the eighth month [14 September], thePrinceofZhao[趙王],[Cao] Gan, died. On the Jiayin day,2there was another order for theGrand Generalto be advanced in peerage toDukeof Jin [晉公] and given the position ofChancellor of State; the courtesy of the esteemed bestowments was prepared. This was like a previous edict. He firmly declined once more, putting this to anend.•••
A Jiayin day does not seem to appear in the eighth month, so this is likely an error. There is a Jiayin day that exists in the ninth month, which would be the date of 20 October, but it is not known if this is correct.↩
***
In the second month of the third year [262], spring, an azure dragon was seen in a well inZhi county[軹縣].
In the fourth month, summer,Liaodong commandery[遼東郡] said that the Sushen state sent envoys, by means of pivot language, to present tribute, that they presented thirty bows that were threechiand fivecunlong, jujube arrow shafts that were onechiand eightcunlong, three hundred stone arrowheads, an assortment of twenty armor made of leather, bone, and iron, and four hundred clothes of sable fur from their state.
In the tenth month, winter, a major general ofShu,Jiang Wei, invadedTaoyang[洮陽]. TheGeneral who Guards the West,Deng Ai, resisted, and he defeated[Jiang] WeiatHouhe[侯和].[Jiang] Weifled. That year, an edict offered sacrifices to the former ArmyLibationer,Guo Jia, at the ancestral temple of theGrand Progenitor.
***
In the second month of the fourth year [263], spring, there was another order for theGrand Generalto be advanced in rank and peerage, as well as being bestowed gifts. This was like a previous edict. He firmly declined once more, putting this to an end.
In the fifth month, summer, an edict stated:
“Shuis but a miniscule, petty nation. Their lands are narrow and their people are few, yetJiang Weiforcibly uses his army, never once conceding his ambitions. After his defeat in the previous year, he still hasTazhong[沓中] tilled, being harsh to the various Qiang. The labor is without an end that the people cannot bear to live. Annexing the weak and attacking the ignorant is a good model in warfare; luring others and not going to them is a superior strategy in military schools. WhoShurelies on is only[Jiang] Wei; there is no one else. We should take advantage of his far distance and separation from his nest! It will be easy to use force now! TheGeneral who Conquers the West,Deng Ai, shall be hereby sent to command various armies to move towardsGansong[甘松] andTazhong[沓中] to capture[Jiang] Wei, and theInspectorofYong province[雍州刺史],Zhuge Xu, shall supervise various armies to move towards Wudu [武都] and Gaolou [高樓]3chasing and conquering them from head to tail. Should[Jiang] Weibe captured, then the two forces shall both advance, thus eradicatingBa-Shu[巴蜀].”•••
Other historical passages have the locations recorded as Wuwei [武街] and Qiaotou [橋頭], so Wudu [武都] and Gaolou [高樓] are possibly errors.↩
TheGeneral who Guards the West,Zhong Hui, was also ordered to attackShufrom Luo valley [駱谷].
In the ninth month, autumn, theGrand Commandant,Gao Rou, died.
On the Jiayin day of the tenth month [9 December], winter, there was another order for theGrand Generalto be advanced in rank and peerage, as well as being bestowed gifts. This was like a previous edict. On the Guimao day [28 November],Lady Bianwas installed asAugust Empress.
In the eleventh month, there was a grand amnesty. WhenDeng AiandZhong Huiled troops to conquerShu, they would triumph wherever they went.
That month, the Lord ofShu,Liu Shan, surrendered to[Deng] Ai, completely pacifyingBa-Shu[巴蜀].
On the Gengxu day of the twelfth month [3 February 264], theExcellency over the Masses,Zheng Chong, becameGrand Protector. On the Renzi day [5 February 264],Yi province[益州] was divided to formLiang province[梁州]. On the Guichou day [6 February 264], the people ofYi province[益州] were given a special pardon and half of their land tax was waived for five years. On the Yimao day [8 February 264], theGeneral who Conquers the West,Deng Ai, becameGrand Commandant, and theGeneral who Guards the West,Zhong Hui, becameExcellency over the Masses. TheAugust Empress Dowagerdied.
***
On the Renxu day of the first month [15 February] of the first year of theera [264], a caged cart was used to summonDeng Ai. On the Jiazi day [17 February],Chang’an[長安] was graced [by theEmperor]. On the Renshen day [25 February], messengers were sent to sacrifice jade rings and silk toMount Hua[華山].
That month,Zhong Huirebelled in Shu [蜀], but was suppressed by troops.Deng Aiwas also killed.
On the Xinmao day of the second month [15 March], there was a special pardon for all those in the lands ofYi[益]. On the Gengshen day [13 April], theBrightandInauguralEmpressGuowas buried.
On the Dingchou day of the third month [30 April], theExcellency of Works,Wang Xiang, was madeGrand Commandant, theGeneral who Conquers the North,He Zeng, was madeExcellency over the Masses, and the LeftVice Secretariat Minister,Xun Yi, was madeExcellency of Works. On the Jimao day [2 May], theDukeof Jin[晉公] was advanced in peerage to King and given ten commanderies which merged with the previous to make twenty.AOn the Dinghai day [10 May],Liu Shanwas enfeoffed asDukeofAnle[安樂公].
On the Gengshen day of the fifth month [12 June], summer, theChancellor of State, theKing ofJin, petitioned to reinstate the Five Ranks peerage. On the Jiaxu day [26 June], the era name was changed. On the Guiwei day [5 July], theCivilandCommandingMarquisofWuyang[舞陽宣文侯] was posthumously made theCommanding KingofJin[晉宣王] and theLoyalandMartialMarquisofWuyang[舞陽忠武侯] was posthumously made theRevered KingofJin[晉景王].
In the sixth month, theGeneral who Guards the West,Wei Guan, petitioned that the troops ofYong province[雍州] received a jade disc and a jade seal each inChengdu county[成都縣]. The seal inscription appeared to be the words “accomplishing sincerity” [成信], which was in accordance with the meaning of theAccomplishedKing ofZhou[周成王] presenting bountiful crops. They were announced and showed to the officials, then stored in the mansion of theChancellor of State.BAfterShuwas pacified, as theWuenemies garrisoned to put pressure onYong’an[永安], the armies ofJing[荊] andYu[豫] were sent to make a two horned maneuver to relieve it.
In the seventh month, thetraitorsall retreated.
On the Gengyin day of the eighth month [10 September], it was ordered that theArmy Comforter of the Center,Sima Yan, would assist theChancellor of Statein affairs. This was akin to the meaning of the Duke of Lu [魯公] being bestowed later.4On the Guisi day [13 September], an edictstated:•••
This is referencing a quote from the Gongyang commentary, “The Duke of Zhou was bestowed first; the Duke of Lu was bestowed later.” [周公拜乎前，魯公拜乎後。] The Duke of Lu was the son of the famousDuke of Zhouand was given the fief by him.↩
TheChronicles of Han and Jinstates: When theDukeof Jin[晉公] advanced in peerage to become King, theGrand Commandant,Wang Xiang; theExcellency over the Masses,He Zeng; and theExcellency of Works,Xun Yi, all went to him.[Xun] Yisaid, “The glory of theChancellor-Kingis immense.Marquis Heand the ministers of the court have all already shown their reverence for him, so today, we should go and pay our respects. There is nothing to be hesitant about.”[Wang] Xiangsaid, “The rank ofChancellor of Stateis mighty, it is indeed glorious and prestigious. However, he is the chancellor ofWei. We are the Three Excellencies ofWei. TheThree Excellenciesand King are separated from each other by just one rank. With the standings being very similar, how could the Three Excellencies under theSon of Heavenbe able to pay respects to others? It would damage the prestige of theWeicourt and diminish the virtue of theKing ofJin. The noble man should love others according to etiquette. So I shall not be doing this.” When they entered,[Xun] Yipaid his respects. It was only[Wang] Xiangwho saluted high. TheKingtold[Wang] Xiang, “From this day onwards, I now know why you are held in great standing!”
Sun Shengstates: In the days of yore, when Gongsun Shu [公孫述] rose up inJinyong[成都], he titled [his nation] Cheng [成]. The texts of the two jades were probably created by [Gongsun] Shu.
“In the past, the treacherous servant,Zhong Hui, plotted rebellion and division. He assembled the conquering officers, compelling them through military force, then began to spit out treacherous plans, speaking of violent insurrection. He coerced the soldiers to make them all to deliberate; experiencing such rapid developments, there was no one among them who was not terrified. The LeftMajorunder theChancellor of State,Xiahou He, and a member of the Cavalry Bureau,Zhu Fu, were ordered to be inChengdu[成都] at the time, while theMajorunder theArmy Leader of the Center,Jia Fu, andPalace GentlemanYang Xiupartook in the military affairs that[Zhong] Huiled.[Xiahou] He,[Yang] Xiu, and[Zhu] Fuall held onto their integrity and did not falter. They opposed the vile words of[Zhong] Hui, and even when they were facing danger, they did not turn away. In their words and gestures, they were righteous and ardent.[Jia] Futold Lower GeneralWang Qi, ‘[Zhong] Huiis evil, rebellious, brutish, and violent. He means to kill every officer.’ He also said, ‘TheChancellor of Stateis leading thirty thousand troops westward to suppress[Zhong] Hui!’ He intended to inflate the situation of power to galvanize the hearts of the troops. When[Wang] Qileft, he told the words of[Jia] Futo the army, thus causing the officers to feel revitalized. It is appropriate to award them with distinction and favor to display their loyalty and righteousness. Thus,[Xiahou] Heand[Jia] Fushall be advanced to peerages, being madeTownship Marquis, and[Yang] Xiuand[Zhu] Fushall be given peerages, being madeMarquis within the Passes.[Wang] Qipromulgated the words of[Jia] Fuand informed them to the officers. This is something proper of an extraordinary reward.[Wang] Qishall hereby be madeUnit General.”
On the Guimao day [23 September], theGeneral of the Guards,Sima Wang, becameGeneral of Coursers.
On the Wuwu day of the ninth month [8 October], theArmy Comforter of the Center,Sima Yan, was madeGrand General who Comforts the Army. On the Xinwei day [21 October], an edict stated:
“The governance and punishments under theWutraitors are brutish and tyrannical, their land taxes being without a limit.Sun Xiueven sent a messenger,Deng Ju, to order theGrand AdministratorofJiaozhi[交阯太守] to confine and send over his people, dispatching them as troops. TheWugeneral,Lü Xing, on the account that the people were feeling outraged, and also because the royal host had pacifiedBa-Shu[巴蜀], assembled powerful people to execute and eliminate[Deng] Ju, among others. He then chased away theGrand Administratorand local chief officials to placate the people in order to wait for our imperial orders. WhenJiuzhen[九真郡] andRinancommanderies [日南郡] heard that[Lü] Xingleft the insurrection and joined the submission, they were of the same heart and echoed in response, allying with[Lü] Xing.[Lü] Xingsent out a letter to theRinan[日南] leaders, revealing his grand plan, and had his army go beforeHepu[合浦], informing them about disaster and fortune. He sentChief CommandantTang Pu, among others, toJincheng county[進乘縣] and petitioned to explain himself through theCommander of the SouthlandsandArmy Protector,Huo Yi. In addition, the generals and officials ofJiaozhi[交阯] petitioned and stated, ‘[Lü] Xinghas begun a meritorious mission, his commands accepted by people major and minor. There are mountain bandits in the commandery that ravage other commanderies. We fear their plans are irregular, that they each harbor treachery. As a expedient and timely measure,[Lü] Xingshould serve asMarshalof various military affairs inJiaozhi[交阯],Upper Grand General, andCounty MarquisofDing’an[定安縣侯]. We beseech for him to be awarded praises and prizes, as this would comfort the desolate border regions.’ Their hearts were sincere and candid as such, shown in their statement. In past times, Yifu [儀父] visited Lu [魯], which was praised by theChronicles,5andDou RongjoinedHan, for which he was treated with extraordinary respect.6TheChronicles, which is highly regarded to have been made in Lu [魯], praises Yifu [儀父], the Duke of Zhu [邾公], for paying homage to it. At present, the distant lands reverberate from our national might, and the six directions are being soothed and comforted by us. We are embracing remote and marginalized areas, amalgamating the four directions.[Lü] Xinghas went headfirst towards our royal influence, having his army bow in submission. He has rushed across ten thousand miles towards righteousness and requested to serve as an official and to adhere to his duties. He should be awarded with lavish treatment and honored with peerage and titles. If we can cause[Lü] Xingand the others to cherish loyalty and move them to joy, then when people afar hear of this, they will certainly all compete with each other for encouragement. Thus,[Lü] Xingshall serve asMessenger Bearing Tally,Commanderof various military affairs inJiao province[交州], andGrand General of the Southlands, and be enfeoffed asCounty MarquisofDing’an[定安縣侯]. He may undertake matters according to convenience, acting first and petitioninglater.”•••
TheChronicles, which is highly regarded to have been made in Lu [魯], praises Yifu [儀父], the Duke of Zhu [邾公], for paying homage to Lu [魯].↩
Dou Rongwas a powerful military leader who had control of the Hexi corridor. He was a subordinate of Wei Xiao [隗囂], but later surrendered to theHanwillingly.↩
This order had not yet arrived when[Lü] Xingwas killed by his subordinates.
On the Dinghai day of the tenth month [6 November], winter, an edict stated:
“In the past, sagacious emperors and enlightened lords would sedate chaos and save the world to preserve their greatness and establish glory. Although civil and military methods are of different paths, the merits and achievements of both converged. Therefore, some brandished weaponry to lecture the disobedient, others organized divisions and brigades to show their might to the arrogant. When it comes to loving the people and preserving the state, giving peace and benevolence to a myriad of beings, then one must first cultivate civil teachings and display guidelines and propriety. Only when there is no other choice can they use the military afterwards. This is a common method of those of immense virtue. In the past, whenTerminal Hanwas fracturing and the Nine Lands [九土] were overturned,Liu BeiandSun Quanexploited the division to create chaos. The Three Progenitors placated the Central Lands [中夏], but being busy without any free time each day, this caused the illegitimate insurrections of the ravaging enemies to last several generations. We were blessed to have the backing of the mighty spirits from the ancestral temples and the loyal martial strength of the chancellors and ministers. Thus, when we dispatched [armies all over] the four directions, we stabilized theYong-Shu[庸蜀]. The military service did not last even one season when we conquered in one expedition! Not long ago, the outer regions of theYangzi River[江表] went into decline, their authority and punishments falling into scramble, and withBa-Han[zhong][巴漢] pacified, they are isolated and imperiled, having no outside aid. TheJiao[交],Jing[荊],Yang[揚], and Yue [越] regions are being swayed towards our wind. At present, the illegitimate general ofJiaozhi[交阯],Lü Xing, has already led his three commanderies and travelled ten thousand miles to submit, the county leaders ofWuling[武陵],Xiang Yanand others, conjoined their five counties and requested to serve as our servants, and the mountain people ofYuzhang[豫章] andLuling[廬陵] have raised their troops in rebellion againstWuand have titled themselves as the General who Aids the North. In addition,Sun Xiudied of illness, changing their leadership. There is defiance within their nation, each man having his own will. The illegitimate general,Shi Ji, is a renowned minister of thetraitors, yet there are suspicions and he is being doubted, and as such, he has received much spite. Their troops are revolting against them, their companions are abandoning them, and no one has any staunch motivation. From antiquity to the present, never has there been such drastic decay. If we have the six armies quake [like thunder] and flash [like lightning], going southward to face theYangzi River[江] andHan River[漢], then it is certainly reasonable for the territories of the Wu-Kuai region [吳會] will certainly support their elderly and carry their young to welcome the royal army. However, even if we dispatch a huge force, there will be labor and financial expenditures. We should inform them of our might and our virtue and display our benevolence and sincerity, so as to cause them to know the advantages of submission and unity. When aMilitary Adjutantunder theChancellor of State,Xu Shao, and abureaucratof the Naval Bureau,Sun Yu, were formerly inShouchun[壽春], they both became our prisoners of war.[Xu] Shaowas originally the illegitimateMarshalofNanling[南陵督], his talent and character showing vigor, and[Sun] Yuwas a kinsman toSun Quan, seeing the situation with a loyal and pleasant nature. We shall send[Xu] Shaoback to the south with[Sun] Yuas his assistant. They shall proclaim our imperial orders and make announcements to the people ofWu. The words that they preach must all be according to the situation and to the truth. If they come to their senses, then it would not disrupt our plan for conquest, for formulating winning strategies in court and making extensive calculations have been methods practiced since antiquity.[Xu] Shaoshall be appointedCavalier Regular Attendant, given the title ofCommandant who Receives the Chariot, and enfeoffed asMarquis of a Capital District, while[Sun] Yushall be madePalace ServantandAttending Gentleman at the Yellow Gates, and conferred peerage ofMarquis within the Passes. The concubines awarded to them and their male and female family members that are here are all allowed to follow him. This is to exemplify the grace of our nation. They do not need to return. This is to display our great sincerity.”
On the Bingwu day [25 November], theGrand General who Comforts the Armyand theTownship Marquisof Xinchang [新昌鄉侯],[Sima] Yan, was ordered to serve as Heir Childe ofJin.
That year, the Field Garrison officials were removed in order to equalize government service. The Officials who Organizes Agriculture were all madeGrand Administratorsand theChief Commandantswere all madePrefectsandChiefs. Those who urged and recruited people from Shu [蜀] and could make them move inward were given food salaries for two years and exempted from taxation for twenty years.Anmi[安彌縣] andFulucounties [福祿縣] each stated that they had a bountiful harvest.
***
On the Jiachen day of the second month [23 March] of the second year [265], spring,Qurun county[朐䏰縣] captured a spirit turtle and presented it. It was sent to the mansion of theChancellor of State. On the Gengxu day [29 March], becauseRapid TigerZhang Xiu, who was inChengdu[成都] in the past, urged his horse to the military camps, told them about the insurrection ofZhong Hui, and eventually lost his life, the younger brother of[Zhang] Xiu,[Zhang] Yi, was conferred peerage ofMarquis within the Passes.
In the fourth month, summer,Nanshenze county[南深澤縣] claimed that sweet dew fell.Wusent envoysJi ZhiandHong Qiuto seek harmony.
In the fifth month, an edict stated:
“TheChancellor of State, theKing ofJin, has spread his divine intelligence vastly, and his radiance has enveloped the lands within theFour Seas. His military achievements quakes [like thunder] and flashes [like lightning], and so his prestige hovers over far away and remote lands. He disseminates refinement and encourages reform, and so he enriches everything without anything outside. He has felt compassion for the outer regions of theYangzi River[江表], striving to preserve them and helping to nurture them. He has ceased warfare and instead advocates benevolence, showing his prestige and virtue. With the written announcements that were delivered, they accepted his refinement and expressed their admiration, sending envoys to present tribute to show that they were obeying him. Through local treasures and exquisite trinkets, they showed their intentions with gladness. Yet, theKing, in his utmost humility and deference, had them all registered and sent here. This is not the way to console and aid the recent surrenderees, nor follow their sincere desires. The various offerings thatSun Haohave sent shall all be sent back and handed to theKing, being in harmony with the principles of antiquity.”
TheKingfirmly declined, putting this to an end. Another command forKing ofJinawarded him a crown with twelve tassels, established the banners of theSon of Heavenfor him, dispatched police when he went out and cleared the roads and prohibited traffic when he went back, awarded him a gilded wooden chariot with six horses, prepared accompanying five-colored vehicles, installed the Maotou and Yunhan guards, awarded eight rows of musicians and dancers, and awarded bell racks for hanging. The Royal Consort was promoted to Royal Queen, the Heir Childe was promoted toHeir Apparent, and the peerages and titles of Royal Sons, Royal Daughters, and Royal Grandsons were all according to past etiquette. On the Guiwei day [30 June], there was a grand amnesty.
On the Xinmao day of the eighth month [6 September], autumn, theChancellor of State, theKing ofJin, died. On the Renchen day [7 September], theHeir ApparentofJin,[Sima] Yan, inherited the fief and succeeded the position, presiding over the officials. The ritual objects and conferment documents were all like those of the past. That month,Xiangwu county[襄武縣] said that a big man was sighted. He was over threezhangtall,7his feet were threechiand twocunlong,8he had white hair, he wore one layer of yellow clothes, he had a yellow scarf, and he used a cane. He toldWang Shi, a man, “Now there shall be great peace!” On the Yiwei day of the ninth month, there was a grand amnesty. On the Wuwu day [3 October], theExcellency over the Masses,He Zeng, becameAssisting ChancellorofJin. On the Guihai day [8 October], theGeneral of Coursers,Sima Wang, becameExcellency over the Masses, theGrand General who Conquers the East,Shi Bao, becameGeneral of Coursers, and the General whoGrand General who Conquers the South,Chen Qian, becameGeneral of Chariots and Cavalry. On the Yihai day [20 October], theCivil KingofJinwas buried.
On the Gengchen day of the intercalary month [25 October], Kangju and Dayuan presented outstanding horses, which were sent to the mansion of theChancellor of Stateto illustrate his merits of cherishing a myriad of nations and reaching the distant.
On the Renxu day of the twelfth month [4 February 266], the bounty of Heaven was terminated, forever, and the celestial orbits were withJin. An edict had theExcellencies,Ministers, and officials furnish the rites [of abdication], setting up the altar in the southern suburbs. The messenger was tasked with receiving the imperial seal, imperial ribbon, and the enthronement command of theAugust Emperor, thus abdicating the position to theSubsequent King ofJin[晉嗣王], akin toHandoing the same toWei. On the Jiazi day [6 February 266], the messenger was tasked to undertake the edict. Thus, he changed his residence toJinyong castle[金墉城], and then eventually lived inYe[鄴]. At the time, he wastwenty.C•••
Assuming onezhangwas tenchi, and onechiwas 24 centimeters at the time, then he was threezhangtall, 720 centimeters, or over 23.6 feet tall.↩
Assuming onechiwas 24 centimeters at the time and assuming onecunwas 2.5 centimeters at the time, then his feet were about 2.53 feet long.↩
TheGenealogy of the Wei Dynastystates: TheEmperorwas enfeoffed asKingofChenliu[陳留王]. When he was fifty-eight, he died in the first year of theera [302]. His posthumous title was theInauguralAugust Emperor[元皇帝].
